  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それはボードウォーゲームです。",
      "それは雑誌の引き出しゲームです。",
      "それは「モンスター」ゲームです。",
      "それは箱入りセットです。",
      "それは拡張されたゲームです。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それは2つの大きなマップを持っています。",
      "それは970個のカウンターを持っています。",
      "それは交互ターンの「イゴー、ユーゴー」システムを持っています。",
      "それは支配領域がありません。",
      "それはモバイルアサルト戦闘システムを持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それはバルバロッサ作戦をシミュレートすることによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは約千個のカウンターを持つことによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは週にプレイヤーごとに2回の行動機会を持つことによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは供給されていないユニットの移動力と戦闘力が半分になることによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは第2版の「未確認」ソビエトユニットによって特徴づけられます。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それはバルバロッサ作戦、第二次世界大戦中のドイツのソ連への奇襲攻撃をシミュレートするために使用されます。",
      "それはモスクワ、レニングラード、スターリングラードの占領をシミュレートするために使用されます。",
      "それはコーカサス油田と農地の獲得をシミュレートするために使用されます。",
      "それはドイツ軍の進撃とソ連軍の反撃をシミュレートするために使用されます。",
      "それは1943年の主要なソ連軍の攻勢でドイツ軍が長期後退を強いられた状況をシミュレートするために使用されます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それはCommand誌の第27号に掲載されました。",
      "それはCommand誌の第34号に掲載されました。",
      "それは1994年の出版物に掲載されました。",
      "それは1995年の出版物に掲載されました。",
      "それは2012年の出版物に掲載されました。"
    ],

      "それは2012年に作られました。"
    ],

      "それはCompass Gamesによって作られました。"
    ],

      "それはモロトフ・リッベントロップ協定に由来します。",
      "それはモスクワ、レニングラード、スターリングラードの占領に由来します。",
      "それはコーカサス油田と農地の獲得に由来します。",
      "それはドイツ軍の後退に由来します。"

IsA: It is a magazine pull-out game.
HasA: It has an "I Go, You Go" system of alternating turns.
It has no zones of control.
It has a Mobile Assault combat system.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by simulating Operation Barbarossa.
It is characterized by having about a thousand counters.
It is characterized by two opportunities for action per player per week.
It is characterized by units having halved movement and combat factors when unsupplied.
It is characterized by "untried" Soviet units in the second edition.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating Operation Barbarossa, Germany's surprise attack on the Soviet Union during World War II.
It is used for simulating the capture of Moscow, Leningrad and Stalingrad.
It is used for simulating the acquisition of the Caucasus oilfields and agricultural lands.
It is used for simulating German advances and Soviet counterattacks.
It is used for simulating a major Soviet offensive that threw the Germans into a long retreat.
LocatedIn: It is located in Issue 27 of Command.
It is located in Issue 34 of Command.
It is located in a 1994 publication.
It is located in a 1995 publication.
It is located in a 2012 publication.
BuiltBy: It was built by Compass Games.
OriginatedIn: It originated in Germany's surprise attack on the Soviet Union during World War II.
It originated in the Molotov–Ribbentrop Pact.
It originated in the capture of Moscow, Leningrad and Stalingrad.
It originated in the acquisition of the Caucasus oilfields and agricultural lands.
It originated in the German retreat.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Ty Bomba.
It was founded by Don Johnson.
CreatedBy: It was created by Ty Bomba.
It was created by Don Johnson.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Ty Bomba.
It was developed by Don Johnson.
PublishedIn: It was published in Issue 27 of Command (March–April 1994).
It was published in Issue 34 of Command (September 1995).
It was published by Compass Games.
WrittenBy: It was written by Ty Bomba.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Compass Games.
ConsistsOf: It consists of two players controlling German and Soviet forces.
It consists of two large maps.
It consists of 970 counters.
It consists of an "I Go, You Go" system.
It consists of weather determination.
It consists of reinforcement and reserve movement.
It consists of movement and attack phases.
It consists of units with movement and combat factors.
It consists of a Mobile Assault combat system.
It consists of "untried" Soviet units in the second edition.
PartOf: It is part of Issue 27 of Command.
It is part of Issue 34 of Command.
It is part of a 1994 publication.
It is part of a 1995 publication.
It is part of a 2012 publication.
KnownFor: It is known for simulating Operation Barbarossa.
It is known for being a "monster" magazine game.
It is known for its "I Go, You Go" gameplay system.
It is known for its Mobile Assault combat.
It is known for its "untried" Soviet units in the second edition.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with World War II.
It is associated with Germany.
It is associated with the Soviet Union.
It is associated with Ty Bomba.
It is associated with GTX.
It is associated with Command magazine.
It is associated with Mark Simonitch.
It is associated with Don Johnson.
It is associated with Compass Games.
It is associated with David W. Nicholas.
NamedAfter: It is named after the Barbarossa Campaign.
HeldIn: It was held in Issue 27 of Command.
It was held in Issue 34 of Command.
About: It is about Germany's surprise attack on the Soviet Union during World War II.
It is about the capture of Moscow, Leningrad and Stalingrad.
It is about the acquisition of the Caucasus oilfields and agricultural lands.
It is about German advances and Soviet counterattacks.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Molotov–Ribbentrop Pact.
It was influenced by the long supply lines during the German offensive.
It was influenced by the oncoming winter.
It was influenced by Soviet counterattacks.
It was influenced by the tide turning in 1943.
Influenced: It influenced the design of Proud Monster: The Barbarossa Campaign – Death & Destruction: The Russian Front 1942-44.
It influenced the design of Proud Monster Deluxe.
